key_insight: The paper shows that adding retrieval, error tracing, and counterexample generation can make LLM-assisted theorem proving more effective for formally verifying blockchain consensus protocols.
engineering_relevance: This matters because stronger automated verification tools can reduce the expertise and effort needed to validate safety-critical distributed systems such as Bitcoin-like consensus.
method: Upgrade IsabeLLM with Retrieval-Augmented Generation to provide the LLM with better proof context.
Add error tracing and counterexample generation to guide proof repair and debugging.
Update compatibility with the latest Isabelle and Sledgehammer for improved efficiency.
evidence_signal: The two IsabeLLM versions are compared on their ability to complete the formal verification of Bitcoin’s Proof of Work consensus.
watch_item: Results may depend heavily on the specific theorem prover, proof corpus, and consensus target, so broader generalization is not guaranteed.
paper_title: IsabeLLM: Automated Theorem Proving Applied to Formally Verifying Consensus
